data.status: has reopened its doors after a brief renovation
data.short_description: 2nd Bridge Brewing Co. has reopened after renovations at Hilliard Mills. It is now a soccer pub with OEC brews available and extensive drink list plus TVs for gamedays. A new Pub Faire menu is coming soon.

Sahara Coffee Co. is coming to the former Subway space. The modern coffeehouse rooted in Yemeni culture will offer traditional drinks, specialty coffee, and cultural spotlights. Renovations are underway.
Juici Patties is bringing its first CT location to North Park Shopping Center. The Jamaican chain will serve various patties, coco bread, and sides in roughly 2,000 square feet.

data.location: Holyoke Mall at Ingleside (center court, upper level), Holyoke
data.status: has opened a new pop-up eatery
data.short_description: Chick-Fil-A opened a pop-up at Holyoke Mall. It features popular items like chicken sandwiches and mac & cheese until August 31st.
J7 Adventureland indoor playground is coming to Patriot Place next to Wormtown Brewing. The 15,000 sq ft space will feature play structures, arcade, and themed days. This is the second location.

data.status: will be closing its doors at the end of June
data.short_description: Dad’s Doughs mini-doughnut shop is closing at the end of June. The shop opened in November 2024 and sold fresh mini doughnuts and local beverages.
Bliss Matcha & Beyond is coming to the former Boost Bowls space in West Hartford Center. The shop will offer matcha items, bubble tea, soft serve, and desserts. This is their second location.

data.location: The Shops at Stone Bridge, 1991 Highland Avenue, Cheshire
data.short_description: Blu Pointe Prime Steakhouse & Seafood is coming to The Shops at Stone Bridge. The upscale spot will feature prime beef, seafood, and a patio. It is owned by Market Hospitality Group.
A new sandwich shop (Ona's Gourmet) is proposed for the former Stacy's Cleaners at 55 White Avenue. Plans include seating for 30 and are subject to approvals.
Tony Phở Restaurant is proposed for 51 Prospect Street. The Vietnamese spot will focus on pho along with other traditional dishes and has planned seating for 36. Plans subject to approvals.
Ross Dress for Less is coming to the former Joann Fabrics space. The discount retailer will occupy 24,890 square feet in the newly named Ross Plaza with a fall opening planned.
Crunch & Flame is coming to the former Rooster's Chicken & Waffles space. The fast-casual spot will serve Caribbean and American items like island bowls and jerk chicken. No timeline announced.
